Memorandum 
To 
John Giansello 
Date: 
January 19, 1977 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
From 
Secretary of State 
Subject: 
Attached Correspondence 
To follow up on our telephone conversation today, attached is the 
letter from Jerome Fishkin, dated January 13, 1977. 
E. SANDLIN, Auditor 
Political Reform Division 
JES/cs 
Attachment 


J ero m e t= i s-h kin

Enclosed please find the Statement of Organization 
of the SHERIFF'S DEFENSE FUND. 
We were advised 
by telephone that we should file the statement. 
I 
do not believe this is proper and request a review 
and written determination of this advice. 
Sheriff Richard Hongisto of San Francisco, and his 
Undersheriff, have been found in contempt of Court 
and ordered to pay $500 fines. 
They intend to 
appeal. 
A member of the Board of Supervisors 
has indicated he will attempt to have the office of 
Sheriff declared vacant, and may attempt to institute 
impeachment or removal proceedings. 
The funds 
raised by this committee's fund will be used for these 
purposes, that is, attorneys fees, costs, fines. 
They 
will not betised for election campaigns. 
Please review and determine if we should file with the 
FPPC. 
If you rule that we should file, please cite 
the statutes, case law, and/or administrative regulations; 
and set forth the reasoning. 
PS: 
Please return the duplicate copy received-stamped. 
Thank you.

sections.question: Enclosed please find the Statement of Organization of the SHERIFF'S DEFENSE FUND. We were advised by telephone that we should file the statement. I do not believe this is proper and request a review and written determination of this advice. Sheriff Richard Hongisto of San Francisco, and his Undersheriff, have been found in contempt of Court and ordered to pay $500 fines. They intend to appeal. A member of the Board of Supervisors has indicated he will attempt to have the office of Sheriff declared vacant, and may attempt to institute impeachment or removal proceedings. The funds raised by this committee's fund will be used for these purposes, that is, attorneys fees, costs, fines. They will not be used for election campaigns. Please review and determine if we should file with the FPPC. If you rule that we should file, please cite the statutes, case law, and/or administrative regulations; and set forth the reasoning.
sections.question_synthetic: Must the Sheriff's Defense Fund file a Statement of Organization with the FPPC when the funds are being raised for legal defense costs, fines, and appeal proceedings related to contempt of court findings and potential removal proceedings, rather than for election campaigns?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains incoming request letter from January 13, 1977, and a cover memorandum from Secretary of State dated January 19, 1977 forwarding the letter to FPPC. No FPPC response/advice letter is present. Document quality is poor with OCR errors affecting readability. No conclusion or FPPC determination included in this document.

Please review and determine if we should file with the FPPC. If you rule that we should file, please cite the statutes, case law, and/or administrative regulations; and set forth the reasoning.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from attorney Jerome Fishkin seeking written determination from the FPPC regarding whether the Sheriff's Defense Fund must file a Statement of Organization, given that the funds will be used for legal defense and court costs rather than election campaigns.
